docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Manthan

Decision Infrastructure for Trusted Systems

Most systems optimize for performance. Manthan optimizes for trust.

The Problem

AI systems operate in probabilistic generation. Same input produces different outputs. There is no traceability. No enforcement.

This makes them unfit for critical systems.

The Shift

AI operates in generation mode. Manthan operates in decision mode.

Generation

Possibilities

Suggestions

Probabilistic

Decision

Outcomes

Enforcement

Deterministic

What Manthan Enables

Deterministic Execution

Same input always produces the same decision.

Traceability

Every decision is recorded and explainable.

Enforcement

Decisions are executed, not suggested.

System Primitive

Decisions become a core layer of your system.

Explore

Explore the System →

📖 Follow Manthan thinking →

Who This Is For

Engineers, founders, and teams building systems where correctness, auditability, and enforcement are non-negotiable.

Connect

📧 founder@manthan.systems 💻 GitHub 🔗 LinkedIn 🚀 Manthan App

Closing

Manthan is not another AI system. It is the layer that makes decisions in software systems traceable, auditable, and enforceable by design.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: Manthan exposes a deterministic decision API.

docs.title: Endpoint
docs.text: POST /v1/decision

docs.title: Headers
docs.text: Content-Type: application/json

docs.title: Body Schema
docs.text:
{
  "contract_id": "string",
  "contract_version": "string",
  "input": {}
}

docs.title: Field Definitions
docs.text: Field Type Description contract_id string Unique identifier of contract contract_version string Immutable version input object Raw decision input

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
{
  "contract_id": "risk-evaluation",
  "contract_version": "v1.0",
  "input": {
    "amount": 15000,
    "country": "high_risk"
  }
}

docs.title: Execution Flow
docs.text:
flowchart LR

I[Input] --> C[Canonicalization] --> H[Hash] --> D[Decision Engine] --> L[Intelligence] --> O[Output]

classDef main fill:#020617,stroke:#60a5fa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;
class I,C,H,D,L,O main;

docs.title: Success Response
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "reject",
  "reason": "amount threshold exceeded",
  "confidence": 0.98,
  "contract_version": "v1.0",
  "hash": "abc123xyz"
}

docs.title: Field Definitions
docs.text: Field Type Description decision string Final decision reason string Explanation confidence number Computed score contract_version string Version used hash string Deterministic input hash

docs.title: Deterministic Guarantee
docs.text:
For every request:

Same contract + Same input → Same response

docs.title: Invalid Contract
docs.text:
{
  "error": "contract_not_found"
}

docs.title: Invalid Input
docs.text:
{
  "error": "invalid_input"
}

docs.title: Version Mismatch
docs.text:
{
  "error": "contract_version_not_found"
}

docs.title: Constraints
docs.text:
• Contracts MUST be immutable
  • Execution order MUST be fixed
  • No randomness allowed
  • No external state dependency

docs.title: Idempotency
docs.text:
Requests are idempotent:

Same request → Same response → Always

docs.title: Example cURL
docs.text:
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/v1/decision \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "contract_id": "risk-evaluation",
    "contract_version": "v1.0",
    "input": {
      "amount": 15000,
      "country": "high_risk"
    }
  }'

docs.title: System Behavior
docs.text:
• No hidden state
  • No time dependency
  • Fully deterministic execution

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
Manthan API provides:

  • Deterministic decisions
  • Versioned contracts
  • Auditable outputs
  • Enforceable actions

docs.title: Core Principle
docs.text:

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: Manthan is a deterministic decision infrastructure built as layered components with strict execution guarantees.

docs.title: System Flow
docs.text:
flowchart LR

A[Input] --> B[Canonicalization] --> C[Decision Engine] --> D[Intelligence Layer] --> E[Enforcement]

A -.-> N1[Normalize]
B -.-> N2[Evaluate Rules]
C -.-> N3[Score]
D -.-> N4[Execute]

classDef main fill:#020617,stroke:#60a5fa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;
classDef note fill:#020617,stroke:#64748b,stroke-width:1px,color:#9ca3af;

class A,B,C,D,E main;
class N1,N2,N3,N4 note;

docs.title: Execution Pipeline
docs.text:
flowchart LR

I[Input] --> C[Canonicalization] --> H[Deterministic Hash] --> D[Decision Engine] --> L[Intelligence Layer] --> O[Output] --> E[Enforcement]

I -.-> N1[Normalize]
C -.-> N2[Hash]
H -.-> N3[Match Rules]
D -.-> N4[Score]
L -.-> N5[Final Decision]
O -.-> N6[Execute]

classDef main fill:#020617,stroke:#60a5fa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;
classDef note fill:#020617,stroke:#64748b,stroke-width:1px,color:#9ca3af;

class I,C,H,D,L,O,E main;
class N1,N2,N3,N4,N5,N6 note;

docs.title: Decision Graph (Dependency Execution)
docs.text:
flowchart LR

A[Input Check] --> B[Risk Evaluation]
A --> C[Fraud Detection]

B --> D[Decision]
C --> D

D --> E[Final Action]

classDef node fill:#020617,stroke:#a78bfa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;
class A,B,C,D,E node;

docs.title: System Architecture (Layered View)
docs.text:
flowchart LR

subgraph InputLayer["Input Layer"]
I1[Raw Input]
C1[Canonicalization]
end

subgraph Core["Decision Core"]
D1[Decision Engine]
L1[Intelligence Layer]
G1[Decision Graph Engine]
end

subgraph Governance["Governance"]
K1[Contracts]
A1[Audit Log]
end

subgraph Execution["Execution"]
E1[Enforcement]
end

I1 --> C1 --> D1 --> L1 --> E1

D1 --> G1
G1 --> D1

D1 --> K1
K1 --> D1

D1 --> A1

classDef input fill:#020617,stroke:#22c55e,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;
classDef core fill:#020617,stroke:#60a5fa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;
classDef gov fill:#020617,stroke:#f59e0b,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;
classDef exec fill:#020617,stroke:#a78bfa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;

class I1,C1 input;
class D1,L1,G1 core;
class K1,A1 gov;
class E1 exec;

docs.title: Canonicalization
docs.text:
Purpose: Normalize input into a stable format

  • Sort keys
  • Normalize values
  • Remove ambiguity

Output: Stable hash

docs.title: Decision Engine
docs.text:
Purpose: Core decision computation

  • Rule-based
  • Fixed execution order
  • First-match wins
  • No randomness

docs.title: Intelligence Layer
docs.text:
Purpose: Add structured metadata

  • Score
  • Confidence
  • Priority
  • Explanation

docs.title: Decision Graphs
docs.text:
Purpose: Handle multi-step dependencies

  • DAG only
  • Topological execution
  • No cycles

docs.title: Contracts
docs.text:
Purpose: Define decision behavior

  • Versioned
  • Immutable
  • Auditable

docs.title: Enforcement
docs.text:
Purpose: Apply decisions externally

  • GitHub PR blocking
  • API enforcement
  • Workflow control

docs.title: System Properties
docs.text:
• Deterministic execution (no randomness)
  • Immutable contract-based logic
  • Full auditability via append-only logs
  • Graph-based dependency resolution

docs.title: System Guarantee
docs.text: Same Input → Same Output → Always

docs.title: Design Principles
docs.text:
• Determinism over intelligence
  • Explicit over implicit
  • Versioned over mutable
  • Auditability over performance

docs.title: Contracts
docs.text: Contracts define decision behavior.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
intent: "fraud_detection"

inputs:
  amount: number

behavior:
  rules:
    - condition: "amount > 10000"
      action: "reject"

    - condition: "true"
      action: "approve"

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• Deterministic
  • Order matters (top → bottom evaluation)
  • First matching rule applies
  • No ambiguity

docs.title: Guarantees
docs.text:
• Same input → same output
  • No randomness
  • No side effects

docs.title: Core Statement
docs.text: Contracts are the single source of truth for decisions.

docs.title: Decision Engine
docs.text:
The Decision Engine is the core deterministic computation layer of Manthan.

It evaluates canonical input against versioned rules and produces a single, predictable decision.

docs.title: Core Principle
docs.text: Same Input + Same Rules → Same Decision

docs.title: Position in System
docs.text:
flowchart LR

C[Canonical Input] --> R[Rule Evaluation] --> M[Match Resolution] --> D[Decision]

classDef main fill:#020617,stroke:#60a5fa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;
class C,R,M,D main;

docs.title: Input
docs.text: The engine operates ONLY on canonicalized input.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• Deterministic structure
  • Stable ordering
  • No ambiguity

docs.title: Rule Model
docs.text: Rules are defined inside contracts.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
rules:
  - condition: "amount > 10000"
    action: "reject"

  - condition: "country == 'high_risk'"
    action: "reject"

  - condition: "true"
    action: "approve"

docs.title: Execution Rules
docs.text: The engine MUST follow strict deterministic behavior:

docs.title: 1. Fixed Order
docs.text: Rules are evaluated top → bottom

docs.title: 2. First Match Wins
docs.text:
• First TRUE condition → decision
  • Remaining rules ignored

docs.title: 3. No Randomness
docs.text:
• No probabilistic outputs
  • No model inference

docs.title: 4. No Side Effects
docs.text:
• Pure function
  • No external calls

docs.title: Evaluation Flow
docs.text:
flowchart LR

A[Rule 1] -->|true| D[Decision]
A -->|false| B[Rule 2]
B -->|true| D
B -->|false| C[Rule 3]
C --> D

classDef main fill:#020617,stroke:#60a5fa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;
class A,B,C,D main;

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
{
  "amount": 12000,
  "country": "low_risk"
}

docs.title: Evaluation
docs.text:
• Rule 1 → TRUE
  • Decision = reject
  • Execution stops

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "reject",
  "reason": "amount threshold exceeded"
}

docs.title: Deterministic Properties
docs.text: Property Guarantee Execution order Fixed Rule evaluation Deterministic Output Stable State Stateless

docs.title: Constraints
docs.text:
The Decision Engine MUST:

  • Use canonical input
  • Execute rules in fixed order
  • Stop at first match
  • Produce exactly one decision

docs.title: What It Does NOT Do
docs.text:
• No learning
  • No probabilistic scoring
  • No external API calls
  • No hidden state

docs.title: System Role
docs.text:
The Decision Engine:

  • Defines the final decision
  • Acts as the system authority
  • Guarantees determinism

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: The Decision Engine is a pure deterministic rule evaluator.

docs.title: Core Statement
docs.text: The engine does not infer. It executes rules deterministically.

docs.title: Example Walkthrough
docs.text: This section demonstrates how Manthan produces deterministic decisions using real scenarios.

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
{
  "amount": 15000,
  "country": "high_risk",
  "user_verified": false
}

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
IF amount > 10000 → reject
IF country == high_risk → reject

docs.title: Decision Flow
docs.text:
• amount > 10000 → TRUE
  • First match → reject

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "reject",
  "reason": "amount threshold exceeded"
}

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
{
  "amount": 2000,
  "country": "low_risk",
  "user_verified": true
}

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
IF amount > 10000 → reject
ELSE → approve

docs.title: Decision Flow
docs.text:
• amount > 10000 → FALSE
  • No reject rule matched → approve

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "approve",
  "reason": "within safe limits"
}

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
{
  "user_age": 17,
  "document_verified": false
}

docs.title: Graph
docs.text:
flowchart LR

A[Age Check] --> B[Eligibility]
A --> C[Document Verification]

B --> D[Decision]
C --> D

classDef node fill:#020617,stroke:#a78bfa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;
class A,B,C,D node;

docs.title: Logic
docs.text:
• user_age < 18 → not eligible
  • document_verified = false → fail

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "reject",
  "reason": "age and verification failure"
}

docs.title: Contract (v1.0)
docs.text:
rules:
  - condition: "score < 50"
    action: "reject"
  - condition: "score >= 50"
    action: "approve"

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
{
  "score": 72
}

docs.title: Evaluation
docs.text:
• score < 50 → FALSE
  • score >= 50 → TRUE

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "approve",
  "contract_version": "v1.0"
}

docs.title: Determinism Proof
docs.text:
For ALL examples:

Same Input → Same Rules → Same Output

No randomness No ambiguity No hidden state

docs.title: Key Takeaway
docs.text:
Manthan converts:

Inputs → Rules → Decisions

Into a fully deterministic system.

docs.title: Decision Graphs
docs.text:
# Decision Graphs

Defines dependency-based execution.

---

## Graph


flowchart LR



A\[Input] --> B\[Risk]

A --> C\[Fraud]

B --> D\[Decision]

C --> D



classDef main fill:#020617,stroke:#a78bfa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;

class A,B,C,D main;

---

## Rules

- DAG only

- No cycles

---

## Guarantee

Deterministic dependency execution

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
# How It Works

Manthan executes decisions through a **deterministic pipeline**.

---

## End-to-End Flow


flowchart LR



A\[Input] --> B\[Canonicalization] --> C\[Decision Engine] --> D\[Intelligence Layer] --> E\[Enforcement]



classDef main fill:#020617,stroke:#60a5fa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;

class A,B,C,D,E main;

---

## Step 1 — Canonicalization

**Goal:** Remove ambiguity from input

### What happens:

- Keys are sorted

- Values are normalized

- Structure is stabilized

### Output:

A **canonical representation** of input

Same logical input → Same canonical form

---

## Step 2 — Deterministic Hash

**Goal:** Create stable identity for input

### What happens:

- Canonical input → hashed

- Same input → same hash

- Different input → different hash

### Output:

A **deterministic identifier**

---

## Step 3 — Decision Engine

**Goal:** Compute decision using rules

### Rules:

- Fixed execution order

- First-match wins

- No randomness

### Example:

IF amount > 10000 → reject

ELSE → approve

### Output:

A **deterministic decision**

---

## Step 4 — Intelligence Layer

**Goal:** Add structured metadata

### Adds:

- Score

- Confidence

- Priority

- Explanation

### Important:

This layer **DOES NOT change the decision**

---

## Step 5 — Decision Graph (if applicable)

**Goal:** Handle multi-step dependencies

### Properties:

- DAG only

- No cycles

- Topological execution

### Example:


flowchart LR



A\[Input Check] --> B\[Risk Evaluation]

A --> C\[Fraud Detection]



B --> D\[Decision]

C --> D



classDef node fill:#020617,stroke:#a78bfa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;

class A,B,C,D node;

---

## Step 6 — Enforcement

**Goal:** Apply decision externally

### Examples:

- Block GitHub PR

- Reject API request

- Trigger workflow

### Output:

**Real-world effect**

---

## Determinism Guarantee

Manthan guarantees:

**Same Input → Same Output → Always**

---

## Why This Matters

Without determinism:

- Decisions cannot be trusted

- Systems cannot be audited

- Outcomes cannot be enforced

With Manthan:

- Every decision is traceable

- Every output is predictable

- Every action is enforceable

---

## Execution Summary


flowchart LR



I\[Input] --> C\[Canonicalization] --> H\[Hash] --> D\[Decision Engine] --> L\[Intelligence] --> E\[Enforcement]



classDef main fill:#020617,stroke:#60a5fa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;

class I,C,H,D,L,E main;

---

## Core Principle

Determinism is not a feature.

It is the foundation.

docs.title: Intelligence Layer
docs.text: Adds metadata without affecting decisions.

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    D[Decision] --> I[Intelligence] --> O[Output]

    classDef main fill:#020617,stroke:#60a5fa,stroke-width:2px,color:#ffffff;
    class D,I,O main;

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "reject",
  "score": 0.92,
  "confidence": "high"
}

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Enriches decision output with metadata
  • Does not modify or override the decision
  • Operates deterministically

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• MUST NOT change decision
  • Stateless
  • Deterministic

docs.title: Boundaries
docs.text:
• No decision authority
  • No side effects
  • No persistence

docs.title: Core Statement
docs.text: Explains decisions, never makes them.

docs.title: System
docs.text:
System

How decisions are made in Manthan.

The Problem

AI systems are probabilistic.

The same input can produce different outputs.

There is no traceability.

There is no enforcement.

This makes them unreliable for decisions.

Generation vs Decision

AI operates in generation mode.

Manthan operates in decision mode.

Prompts generate possibilities.

Contracts define outcomes.

Generation is not decision-making.

Decision Boundary

AI is useful for:

  • Ambiguity
  • Interpretation
  • Exploration

Decisions require:

  • Determinism
  • Enforcement
  • Guarantees

Crossing the boundary turns insight into decision.

Architecture

Decisions originate from human intent.

Intent is defined as contracts.

Contracts are executed by Manthan.

Execution produces deterministic decisions.

Decisions are enforced by the system.

Contracts

  • Versioned
  • Immutable
  • Deterministic

Contracts are the source of truth.

Decisions are not inferred.

They are specified.

Intelligence Layer

Decisions are made first.

  • Explanation
  • Context
  • Scoring

It does not influence the decision.

It explains decisions, never makes them.

Outcome

Without deterministic decisions:

  • Inconsistent outcomes
  • No traceability
  • No enforcement

With Manthan:

  • Consistent decisions
  • Full traceability
  • Enforced outcomes

Reliable systems require deterministic decisions.

docs.text:
